title: Mastering Diverse Domains through World Models
topic_signature: world model, model-based reinforcement learning, DreamerV3, recurrent state-space model, latent imagination, diverse domains, RSSM, actor-critic
abstract_summary: DreamerV3 is a general model-based reinforcement learning algorithm that learns a world model from experience and trains an actor-critic policy entirely from imagined trajectories. It outperforms specialized methods across over 150 diverse tasks using a single fixed set of hyperparameters, marking the first time a single algorithm solves domains across different observation modalities, action spaces, and reward structures without per-domain tuning. Published in Nature, it represents a landmark in scalable model-based RL.

title: Genie: Generative Interactive Environments
topic_signature: generative interactive environment, unsupervised learning, internet video, action control, latent action model, foundation world model, 11B parameters, video generation
abstract_summary: Genie is the first generative interactive environment trained in an unsupervised manner from unlabelled Internet videos. At 11B parameters, it can be prompted with text, images, photographs, or sketches to generate an endless variety of action-controllable virtual worlds, inferring latent actions that enable frame-by-frame user interaction despite training without ground-truth action labels. It represents a paradigm shift toward foundation world models that learn controllable environment dynamics from passive video data.

title: Sora: A Review on Background, Technology, Limitations, and Opportunities of Large Vision Models
topic_signature: Sora, video generation, world simulator, diffusion model, spacetime patches, transformer architecture, text-to-video, emergent physical simulation, OpenAI
abstract_summary: This paper provides a comprehensive review of OpenAI's Sora model, which positions video generation models as world simulators by training text-conditional diffusion models on variable-duration videos and images using a transformer architecture operating on spacetime patches. Sora demonstrates emergent capabilities including physics simulation, 3D consistency, and object permanence when generating up to one minute of high-fidelity video from text prompts. The review covers the model's background, underlying technologies (diffusion transformers, latent space compression), limitations, and the broader implications for world modeling.

title: TD-MPC2: Scalable, Robust World Models for Continuous Control
topic_signature: model-based reinforcement learning, continuous control, implicit world model, multi-task learning, trajectory optimization, scalable, robust, decoder-free, MPC
abstract_summary: TD-MPC2 is a scalable model-based RL algorithm that performs trajectory optimization in the latent space of a learned implicit (decoder-free) world model across 104 continuous control tasks. It introduces a series of improvements over TD-MPC including better scalability, robust multi-task training, and a unified set of hyperparameters, outperforming existing model-free and model-based methods. The work demonstrates that implicit world models can be scaled effectively to handle diverse control domains simultaneously.

title: Diffusion for World Modeling: Visual Details Matter in Atari
topic_signature: diffusion world model, DIAMOND, Atari, visual details, reinforcement learning, score-based diffusion, environment simulation, discrete vs continuous latent
abstract_summary: DIAMOND (DIffusion As a Model Of eNvironment Dreams) presents the first RL agent trained entirely in a diffusion-based world model, challenging the prevailing approach of using discrete latent representations. The paper demonstrates that diffusion models preserve fine visual details critical for RL performance that discrete tokenizers lose, achieving strong results on Atari benchmarks. This work establishes diffusion models as a viable alternative paradigm for world modeling in reinforcement learning.

title: Cosmos World Foundation Model Platform for Physical AI
topic_signature: world foundation model, physical AI, video curation, diffusion, autoregressive, fine-tuning, robotics, autonomous driving, NVIDIA, open platform
abstract_summary: Cosmos is a comprehensive world foundation model platform designed to help developers build customized world models for Physical AI applications including robotics and autonomous driving. The platform provides a complete pipeline from video curation through pre-trained foundation models to fine-tuning, offering both diffusion and autoregressive world models. It represents an industry-scale effort to democratize world model development with open models and tooling.

title: Understanding World or Predicting Future? A Comprehensive Survey of World Models
topic_signature: world model survey, taxonomy, understanding vs prediction, autonomous driving, embodied AI, video generation, LLM-based world models, comprehensive review
abstract_summary: This comprehensive survey proposes a taxonomy dividing world models into two paradigms: those that understand the present world state and those that predict future dynamics, covering applications from autonomous driving to robotics and gaming. It systematically reviews the evolution from classical world models through modern LLM-based and video generation approaches, connecting model-based RL and generative AI traditions. Published in ACM CSUR, it serves as the definitive reference for the field's structure and open challenges.

title: DrivingWorld: Constructing World Model for Autonomous Driving via Video GPT
topic_signature: autonomous driving, video GPT, autoregressive world model, spatial-temporal fusion, ego state prediction, long-duration video generation, next-state prediction
abstract_summary: DrivingWorld is a GPT-style autoregressive world model for autonomous driving that jointly generates future video sequences and predicts ego-vehicle states through spatial-temporal fusion mechanisms. The model disentangles temporal and spatial information using novel architectural components including temporal-aware blocks and dropout-for-drifting-free autoregression, enabling high-fidelity, long-duration driving video generation. It demonstrates that GPT-style next-token prediction can be effectively adapted to model complex driving dynamics.

title: RLVR-World: Training World Models with Reinforcement Learning
topic_signature: reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards, RLVR, world model training, tokenized prediction, task-specific optimization, autoregressive world model, MLE misalignment
abstract_summary: RLVR-World proposes a unified framework that uses reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards (RLVR) to directly optimize world models for task-specific metrics, addressing the misalignment between standard maximum likelihood estimation and downstream objectives like transition prediction accuracy and perceptual quality. By formulating world modeling as autoregressive prediction of tokenized state-action sequences, the framework achieves significant improvements across text, web, and video domains. This work bridges the gap between generic world model pretraining and task-specific evaluation.

title: RoboScape: Physics-informed Embodied World Model
topic_signature: physics-informed world model, embodied AI, robotic video generation, depth prediction, keypoint dynamics, multi-task learning, physical plausibility, RGB video generation
abstract_summary: RoboScape is a unified physics-informed world model that jointly learns RGB video generation and physics knowledge—including temporal depth prediction and adaptive keypoint dynamics—within a multi-task autoregressive framework for robotic scenarios. By integrating physical constraints directly into the world modeling pipeline, it generates videos that are both visually realistic and physically plausible, addressing a key limitation of prior world models that ignored physics fidelity. The model improves motion prediction accuracy and 3D geometry understanding in robot manipulation and navigation tasks.

title: World-in-World: World Models in a Closed-Loop World
topic_signature: closed-loop benchmark, world model evaluation, embodied agent, task success, controllability, action-conditioned, decision making, online planning
abstract_summary: World-in-World is the first open benchmarking platform that evaluates generative world models (WMs) by their practical utility for embodied agents in closed-loop interaction, rather than just visual quality. It wraps heterogeneous WMs in a unified closed-loop interface with a standardized action API, curating four environments that rigorously test diverse WMs. The benchmark reveals that controllability and scaling post-training with action-observation data are more effective than visual fidelity for downstream task success, establishing a paradigm shift from open-loop visual evaluation to closed-loop outcome-based assessment of world models.

title: Infinite-World: Scaling Interactive World Models to 1000-Frame Horizons via Pose-Free Hierarchical Memory
topic_signature: interactive world model, 1000-frame memory, pose-free, hierarchical memory compressor, real-world video training, long-horizon coherence, action control, uncertainty-aware
abstract_summary: Infinite-World is a robust interactive world model that maintains coherent visual memory over 1000+ frames in complex real-world environments, far exceeding the horizons of prior interactive world models. Unlike synthetic-data-trained models, it is trained directly on real-world videos without requiring perfect pose annotations, using a Hierarchical Pose-free Memory Compressor (HPMC) and uncertainty-aware action labeling. This enables stable, long-horizon interactive simulation from real-world visual data, a critical capability for scalable embodied AI.

title: Ctrl-World: A Controllable Generative World Model for Robot Manipulation
topic_signature: controllable world model, robot manipulation, video diffusion, multi-view prediction, fine-grained action control, long-horizon interaction, closed-loop evaluation, policy-in-the-loop
abstract_summary: Ctrl-World is a controllable generative world model for robot manipulation adapted from a pretrained video diffusion model. It supports multi-view input and joint prediction, fine-grained action control, and consistent long-horizon interactions via a memory retrieval mechanism. Designed for policy-in-the-loop rollouts with generalist robot policies, it enables closed-loop policy evaluation and improvement entirely within the model's imagination, making it compatible with modern VLA (Vision-Language-Action) frameworks.

title: ResWorld: Temporal Residual World Model for End-to-End Autonomous Driving
topic_signature: temporal residual world model, autonomous driving, BEV representation, dynamic object modeling, end-to-end driving, trajectory planning, temporal residual, static region
abstract_summary: ResWorld introduces a Temporal Residual World Model (TR-World) that isolates dynamic objects by computing temporal residuals of BEV (Bird's Eye View) scene representations, thereby reducing redundant static-object modeling. By predicting only what changes between frames rather than entire scenes from scratch, it achieves more precise future spatial distribution prediction for dynamic objects and state-of-the-art planning performance in end-to-end autonomous driving frameworks.

title: DreamDojo: A Generalist Robot World Model from Large-Scale Human Videos
topic_signature: foundation world model, generalist robot, dexterous manipulation, egocentric human video, 44k hours, distillation, cross-embodiment, action control
abstract_summary: DreamDojo is a foundation world model pretrained on 44,000 hours of egocentric human videos that can simulate diverse dexterous robotics tasks and generalize to unseen objects and environments. It addresses the challenge of limited robotic data coverage by learning diverse interactions and dexterous controls from large-scale passive human video, then transferring to robot-specific tasks via a distillation pipeline. It demonstrates unprecedented generalization to diverse objects and environments after post-training, representing a major step toward generalist robot world models.

title: Gamma-World: Generative Multi-Agent World Modeling Beyond Two Players
topic_signature: multi-agent world model, generative simulation, Simplex Rotary Agent Encoding, Sparse Hub Attention, permutation-symmetric, zero-shot generalization, real-time rollout, 24 FPS
abstract_summary: Gamma-World (γ-World) is a generative multi-agent world model for interactive simulation that supports independently controllable, permutation-symmetric agents via Simplex Rotary Agent Encoding and Sparse Hub Attention. It achieves real-time 24 FPS rollouts and demonstrates zero-shot generalization from two to four players, extending interactive world modeling beyond the two-agent paradigm that dominated prior work. This enables scalable simulation of complex multi-agent dynamics for game environments and training.

title: Physically Native World Models: A Hamiltonian Perspective on Generative World Modeling
topic_signature: Hamiltonian world model, phase space, generalized coordinates, energy function, physically grounded, action-controllable, long-horizon stability, latent dynamics
abstract_summary: This paper proposes Hamiltonian World Models, a physically grounded framework that rethinks generative world modeling through the lens of Hamiltonian dynamics. It encodes observations into structured latent phase spaces with generalized coordinates, momenta, and an energy function, evolving them using Hamiltonian-inspired dynamics. This approach unifies the three separated routes in current world model research (2D video generation, 3D scene-centric models, and JEPA-like latent prediction) into a physically native framework that generates action-controllable and long-horizon stable predictions.

title: Interactive World Simulator for Robot Policy Training and Evaluation
topic_signature: interactive world simulator, robot policy training, physically consistent interaction, scalable data generation, synthetic demonstrations, imitation learning, action-conditioned video prediction
abstract_summary: Interactive World Simulator is a framework for building action-conditioned world models that serve as scalable surrogates for physical robot data collection. It generates high-quality synthetic demonstrations for imitation policy training without requiring access to physical robots, and enables faithful, reproducible policy evaluation. The framework addresses limitations of existing action-conditioned video prediction models that are often slow and struggle to capture physically consistent interactions over long horizons, establishing a stable and physically consistent pipeline for scalable robotic data generation.

title: Chain of World: World Model Thinking in Latent Motion
topic_signature: world model VLA, latent motion, Latent Motion Extractor, visuomotor learning, temporal reasoning, disentangled representation, robotics, action chain
abstract_summary: CoWVLA (Chain of World VLA) unifies the strengths of world-model Vision-Language-Action models and latent-action VLAs. A Latent Motion Extractor decomposes video into structural and motion latent variables, preserving world-model benefits of temporal reasoning and world knowledge while retaining the compactness and interpretability of latent actions. This enables efficient visuomotor learning in robotic systems by combining rich world understanding with compact action representations.

title: X-WIN: Building Chest Radiograph World Model via Predictive Sensing
topic_signature: chest radiograph world model, 3D anatomical representation, CT knowledge distillation, predictive sensing, action-conditioned projection, medical imaging, contrastive alignment, 2D-to-3D
abstract_summary: X-WIN (X-ray World Intelligence Network) is a novel chest radiograph world model that distills volumetric knowledge from chest CT scans by learning to predict 2D projections in latent space under 3D transformations. It overcomes the structural superposition limitations of 2D X-rays through an action-conditioned framework, optimizing representations with affinity-guided contrastive alignment and masked image modeling. X-WIN outperforms existing foundation models on diverse downstream medical imaging tasks using linear probing and few-shot fine-tuning, extending world modeling to medical imaging.

title: DriveWorld-VLA: Unified Latent-Space World Modeling with Vision-Language-Action for Autonomous Driving
topic_signature: unified latent-space world model, autonomous driving, VLA, world modeling, planning, latent dynamics, multi-view fusion, end-to-end driving
abstract_summary: DriveWorld-VLA unifies world modeling and planning in a shared latent space for autonomous driving, integrating Vision-Language-Action capabilities. By performing physical reasoning in the latent space rather than pixel space, it jointly models environment dynamics and generates driving actions, bridging the gap between world simulation and action generation. This approach demonstrates that unified latent-space representations can effectively combine the strengths of generative world models and end-to-end planning for autonomous driving.

title: World-VLA-Loop: Closed-Loop Learning of Video World Model and VLA Policy
topic_signature: closed-loop learning, world model, VLA policy, co-evolution, iterative refinement, failure rollout, action-following precision, reinforcement learning
abstract_summary: World-VLA-Loop is a closed-loop framework that establishes a co-evolving cycle between world model learning and VLA (Vision-Language-Action) policy optimization through iterative refinement. It uses failure rollouts from the policy to refine the world model's action-following precision, which in turn improves downstream reinforcement learning performance. This bidirectional feedback loop addresses the chicken-and-egg problem of needing good world models for policy training and good policies for world model evaluation.

title: Video Generation Models as World Models: Efficient Paradigms, Architectures and Algorithms
topic_signature: video generation survey, efficient world model, modeling paradigms, network architectures, inference algorithms, real-time, autonomous driving, embodied AI
abstract_summary: This survey comprehensively reviews efficient video generation frameworks for world modeling, introducing a novel three-dimensional taxonomy: efficient modeling paradigms, efficient network architectures, and efficient inference algorithms. It argues that efficiency is a fundamental prerequisite for evolving video generators into general-purpose, real-time, and robust world simulators. The survey examines how these efficiency-oriented methods directly enable interactive applications such as autonomous driving, embodied AI, and game simulation, bridging the gap between research and practical deployment.

title: World Action Models: The Next Frontier in Embodied AI
topic_signature: world action model, embodied AI, unified dynamics modeling, motor control, predictive perception, action generation, foundation model, embodied reasoning
abstract_summary: This position/survey paper introduces World Action Models (WAMs) as a class of embodied foundation models that unify environmental dynamics modeling (world modeling) with motor control (action generation). WAMs represent the next frontier in embodied AI by bridging the traditionally separated concerns of predicting world states and generating physical actions in a single framework. The paper provides a roadmap for combining predictive world-state modeling with action generation, articulating how this unification can enable more capable and general embodied agents.

title: Matrix-Game 3.0: Real-Time and Streaming Interactive World Model with Long-Horizon Memory
topic_signature: real-time interactive world model, streaming generation, long-horizon memory, game environment, action-conditioned, controllable generation, first-person perspective, playable
abstract_summary: Matrix-Game 3.0 is a real-time and streaming interactive world model that supports long-horizon memory for sustained interaction in game-like environments. It advances the Matrix-Game series by enabling streaming generation that maintains temporal coherence and spatial consistency over extended play sessions, addressing the memory bottleneck that limits prior interactive world models. The model supports real-time interactive generation with first-person action control, pushing toward practical playable AI-generated environments.
